subtasks.description: Query SAM database for licensed Microsoft users and their entitlements (count by product, license type, and assigned user)
Query consumption facts warehouse for active Microsoft users and their actual usage metrics (count by product, monthly active users, spend by product line)

Reconcile SAM licensed user counts against consumption active users; calculate licensing gaps (under-provisioned) and excess capacity (over-provisioned) by product; classify posture (compliant, at-risk, over-licensed) and provide optimization recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: two mid-tier SQL queries (one for SAM entitlements, one for consumption actuals—separate sources require separate queries but are independent and can run in parallel), plus one frontier-tier cross_recon to reconcile both datasets, calculate delta metrics (under/over-provisioning gaps), classify licensing posture, and generate optimization recommendations. The frontier tier is justified because reconciliation requires multi-signal analysis (licensed count vs active user count vs spend trends), delta calculation, posture classification, and actionable recommendation generation—all part of the cross_recon output. No separate policy_action is needed; compliance reasoning is embedded in cross_recon classification. The plan minimizes subtasks by consolidating all metrics into two query calls and one intelligent reconciliation step.

output.sql: SELECT c.publisher, c.product, COUNT(DISTINCT c.license_id) as license_count, COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) as active_users, c.event_type as license_type, SUM(c.amount_usd) as total_spend_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT c.daily_active_seats) as seat_utilization FROM consumption_facts c WHERE c.publisher = 'Microsoft' AND c.event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY c.publisher, c.product, c.event_type ORDER BY c.product, c.event_type

SELECT p.product, COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) as active_users, COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('month', c.event_date)) as active_months, COUNT(*) as event_count, SUM(c.amount_usd) as total_spend_usd, SUM(c.daily_active_seats) as total_active_seats FROM consumption_facts c WHERE c.publisher = 'Microsoft' AND c.event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY c.product ORDER BY total_spend_usd DESC
